page: Microsoft Azure L48s_v2 is a storage-optimized virtual machine from the LSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7551 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 12,012 GB of capacity, and it is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core performance is a notable bottleneck, with the stress-ng single-core CPU test scoring in the bottom 10% and Geekbench single-core scoring in the bottom 25%. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also fall into the bottom 25%. Conversely, multi-core and parallelized workloads perform in the average tier. The stress-ng multi-core test, Geekbench multi-core test, and Passmark CPU score all register in the middle 50% of benchmarks. Notably, the Passmark CPU encryption test demonstrates strong performance, placing in the top 25% with a score of 38,237.8.

Memory bandwidth remains consistently average across both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks. Database operations and web serving throughput also align with the middle 50% of tested systems, with Redis SET operations achieving 27.75 million operations per second and static web serving reaching 14.82 GB/sec. Large language model inference is limited, with text generation on a 7B model and prompt processing on a 70B model scoring in the bottom 10%. Given the massive 12,012 GB local storage and high memory-to-core ratio, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy, multi-threaded workloads such as large databases, big data analytics, and transactional processing, where single-core speed is secondary to parallel throughput and local storage density.
description: Microsoft Azure L48s_v2 is an LSv2-family virtual machine configured with 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 384.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7551 processor running at 3.2 GHz. It features a substantial 12,012 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it lacks GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks highlight a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks score in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-core operations, software compilation, and Passmark CPU scores sit firmly in the average tier. Notably, cryptographic performance is strong, ranking in the top 25%. With average memory bandwidth and database operations, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-capacity storage and memory-intensive parallel workloads. It is best suited for large databases, big data analytics, and multi-threaded transactional applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L48s_v2 is a storage-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 12,012 GB of local storage, delivering average multi-core performance but weaker single-core speeds.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L48s_v2 features 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 12,012 GB local storage, offering average multi-core and strong cryptographic performance.
tagline: A storage-dense, memory-rich virtual server featuring high local capacity and balanced multi-core performance for parallelized database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical AMD EPYC cores at 3.2 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory and 12,012 GB of local storage
Strong cryptographic performance ranking in the top 25%
Average multi-core processing and memory bandwidth capabilities
Lower single-core execution speeds and single-threaded performance
Suited for large-scale databases and parallelized storage-heavy workloads
